title: dorstenia houstonii
intro: Dorstenia houstonii, also known as Houston's crocodile bark or Crocodile Bark, is a plant species in the family Moraceae. It is a medicinal plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Caribbean and Central America, for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, Dorstenia houstonii is often used in teas, infusions, and poultices, and is also used as a topical treatment for wounds and skin conditions.
